args.description: A user defined struct that implements the [FFTProcessable](FFTProcess.md/#trait-fftprocessable) trait.
args.name: process

overloads.name: __init__
overloads.signature: __init__(out self, world: UnsafePointer[MMMWorld, MutExternalOrigin], var process: T)

args.description: The next input sample to process.
args.name: input

overloads.name: next
overloads.summary: Processes the next input sample and returns the next output sample.

args.description: The next input samples to process.
args.name: input

overloads.name: next_stereo
overloads.summary: Processes the next stereo input sample and returns the next output sample.

args.description: The input buffer to read samples from.
args.name: buffer

args.description: The current phase to read from the buffer. Between 0 (beginning) and 1 (end).
args.name: phase

args.description: The channel to read from the buffer.
args.name: chan

overloads.name: next_from_buffer
overloads.signature: next_from_buffer(mut self, ref buffer: Buffer, phase: Float64, chan: Int = 0) -> Float64
overloads.summary: Returns the next output sample from the internal buffered process. The buffered process reads a block of samples from the provided buffer at the given phase and channel on each hop.

args.description: The input buffer to read samples from.
args.name: buffer

args.description: The current phase to read from the buffer. Between 0 (beginning) and 1 (end).
args.name: phase

args.description: The first channel to read from the buffer.
args.name: start_chan

overloads.name: next_from_stereo_buffer
overloads.signature: next_from_stereo_buffer(mut self, ref buffer: Buffer, phase: Float64, start_chan: Int = 0) -> SIMD[DType.float64, 2]
overloads.summary: Returns the next stereo output sample from the internal buffered process. The buffered process reads a block of samples from the provided buffer at the given phase and channel on each hop.

structs.name: ComplexFFTProcess

parameters.description: A user defined struct that implements the [FFTProcessable](FFTProcess.md/#trait-fftprocessable) trait.
parameters.name: T

parameters.description: The size of the FFT window.
parameters.name: window_size

parameters.description: The number of samples between each processed spectral frame.
parameters.name: hop_size

parameters.description: Int specifying what window shape to use to modify the amplitude of the input samples before the FFT. See [WindowType](MMMWorld.md/#struct-windowtype) for the options.
parameters.name: input_window_shape

parameters.description: Int specifying what window shape to use to modify the amplitude of the output samples after the IFFT. See [WindowType](MMMWorld.md/#struct-windowtype) for the options.
parameters.name: output_window_shape

structs.signature: struct ComplexFFTProcess[T: ComplexFFTProcessable, window_size: Int = 1024, hop_size: Int = 512, input_window_shape: Int = WindowType.hann, output_window_shape: Int = WindowType.hann]
structs.summary: Create an FFTProcess for audio manipulation in the frequency domain. This version will output and input complex frequency bins directly rather than magnitude and phase. This is currently untested.

aliases.description:
The implementation of `__del__` is considered to be trivial if:
- The struct has a compiler-generated trivial destructor and all its fields
  have a trivial `__del__` method.

In practice, it means that the `__del__` can be considered as no-op.
aliases.name: __del__is_trivial
aliases.summary: A flag (often compiler generated) to indicate whether the implementation of `__del__` is trivial.

aliases.description:
The implementation of `__copyinit__` is considered to be trivial if:
- The struct has a compiler-generated trivial `__copyinit__` and all its fields
  have a trivial `__copyinit__` method.

In practice, it means the value can be copied by copying the bits from
one location to another without side effects.
aliases.name: __copyinit__is_trivial
aliases.summary: A flag (often compiler generated) to indicate whether the implementation of `__copyinit__` is trivial.

aliases.description:
The implementation of `__moveinit__` is considered to be trivial if:
- The struct has a compiler-generated `__moveinit__` and all its fields
  have a trivial `__moveinit__` method.

In practice, it means the value can be moved by moving the bits from
one location to another without side effects.
aliases.name: __moveinit__is_trivial
aliases.summary: A flag (often compiler generated) to indicate whether the implementation of `__moveinit__` is trivial.

traits.description:
See `TestFFTProcess.mojo` for an example on how to create a spectral process 
using a struct that implements FFTProcessable.

overloads.name: __copyinit__
overloads.summary: Create a new instance of the value by copying an existing one.

overloads.name: __moveinit__
overloads.summary: Create a new instance of the value by moving the value of another.

overloads.name: copy
overloads.summary: Explicitly construct a copy of self.

traits.name: ComplexFFTProcessable
traits.summary: Implement this trait in a custom struct to pass to `FFTProcess` as a Parameter.
